cards.name: Saffron
cards.description: Preciousness, intensity in small amounts, the power of restraint.
cards.text: What is the smallest thing you could offer today that might change the colour of everything?

cards.name: Chamomile
cards.description: Calm, gentleness, steadiness that heals.
cards.text: Where might steadiness be more useful than brilliance in this room today?

cards.name: Cardamom
cards.description: Complexity hidden in a small vessel, warmth with surprise.
cards.text: What would it look like to bring your full complexity into the room — not just the parts people expect?

cards.name: Cinnamon
cards.description: Warmth, familiarity, comfort that draws people closer.
cards.text: What would happen if you led with warmth before anything else?

cards.name: Mint
cards.description: Refreshment, clarity, the ability to cut through and wake things up.
cards.text: Where could a moment of sharp honesty open a door that politeness cannot?

cards.name: Star Anise
cards.description: Geometry in nature, beauty in structure, the unexpected shape of things.
cards.text: What pattern keeps showing up in your work that you haven't yet named out loud?

cards.name: Turmeric
cards.description: Staining power, leaving a mark, transformation that cannot be undone.
cards.text: What are you willing to be changed by today — even if it leaves a mark?

cards.name: Lavender
cards.description: Calm before the threshold, preparation, ritual.
cards.text: What ritual — even a tiny one — could help you arrive fully before you begin?

cards.name: Black Pepper
cards.description: Activating energy, the spark that wakes things up, necessary friction.
cards.text: Where are you avoiding the friction that might actually be the thing that moves everyone forward?

cards.name: Bay Leaf
cards.description: Slow release, patience, influence that builds over time.
cards.text: What have you been adding quietly to this work that no one has noticed yet — and does it need to be noticed?

cards.name: Vanilla
cards.description: Depth mistaken for simplicity, the richness of what seems ordinary.
cards.text: The things people call simple are almost never simple. There is an entire journey inside what looks familiar.

cards.name: Sumac
cards.description: Bright acidity, the gift of a shifted perspective, waking up flavour in others.
cards.text: You don't always need to be the main ingredient. Sometimes the most generous thing is to make everyone around you more vivid.

cards.name: Clove
cards.description: Intensity, numbing and healing at once, medicine that stings before it helps.
cards.text: Not everything that helps feels good at first. Some care arrives sharp, and softens only after it has done its work.

cards.name: Basil
cards.description: Freshness, abundance, generosity that grows back when shared.
cards.text: The more you give of this kind of attention, the more of it there seems to be. It replenishes itself.

cards.name: Ginger
cards.description: Underground strength, knotted growth, warmth that builds slowly.
cards.text: The most interesting roots are never straight. They grow around what is in the way, and become stronger for it.

cards.name: Rosemary
cards.description: Remembrance, fidelity, the thread between past and present.
cards.text: There's rosemary, that's for remembrance; pray, love, remember.

cards.name: Sage
cards.description: Wisdom, purification, clearing what no longer serves.
cards.text: The only true wisdom is in knowing you know nothing.

cards.name: Thyme
cards.description: The weight of small moments, time's quiet accumulation.
cards.text: To see a world in a grain of sand, and a heaven in a wild flower.

cards.name: Fennel
cards.description: Licorice duality, sweetness and sharpness coexisting, paradox.
cards.text: Do I contradict myself? Very well then I contradict myself. I am large, I contain multitudes.

cards.name: Nutmeg
cards.description: Hidden interior, the reward of cracking something open, warmth at the centre.
cards.text: The real voyage of discovery consists not in seeking new landscapes, but in having new eyes.
